## reference · mobile onboarding empty state value

* Avoid creating app onboarding whenever possible; instead, make the UI more usable.
* Onboarding is only necessary in a few situations:
  * When user information is needed to get started.
  * When app functionality is highly tailored to the user's context and preferences.
  * When app features or workflows are unique or unfamiliar.
* There are three common components of mobile onboarding flows: 
  * Feature promotion
  * Customization
  * Instructions
* Avoid feature-promotion onboarding at first launch; instead, present it on the app store page or through contextual help within the app.
* Feature onboarding can be helpful to existing users when new features are released, but limit it to what's truly new to users.
* Visual-design customization doesn't belong in onboarding; save it for later.
* Content customization can create a relevant experience and is more likely to be appropriate for initial app onboarding.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/mobile-app-onboarding/
